title: Accessing 'podman machine' API
body:
Hi

Is it possible to somehow interact with the `podman machine` commands through this package?

comments.body:
Hey, not at the moment.

What's your use case?

Would you like to work on the implementation? (I can help with PR reviews or discussions it if you like)

@inknos Thanks for the quick answer.

> What's your use case?

Nothing I cannot solve with the CLI right now, so ultimately I can accept relying on that.


> Would you like to work on the implementation? (I can help with PR reviews or discussions it if you like)

Sounds interesting, I tried to find the API spec, is it this one? https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html
I don't see anything for the `machine`s, can you give some high-level pointers on how to implement this?

The link you provided brings to the docs for the `podman` (or rather `libpod`) API, that's absolutely right.

`podman machine` is not part of it. Why? While `machine` is a core part of `podman`, it also solves specific use cases. I would say that `podman machine` on Linux solves no problem, but it enables us to run containers on Windows and macOS.

So here's the thing. `podman-py` is a package that mostly (or solely? I am not sure) conveniently exposes the `libpod`'s RESTful API to python bindings. TBH I am not sure if `podman-py` does other things which are not part of the API. Although it could, maybe it just does not.

I believe your question is valid, although it has to find a purpose in the implementation, which requires some considerations.

Evaluate a typical use case for `podman machine` on [Windows](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman-for-windows.md#machine-init-process), for example.
The user has to `init`, `start` the machine, then run `podman` from inside the machine (ie interacting with the unix socket, being in the Linux Kernel user space).

Where does `podman-py` fit? Say that you install `podman-py` on Windows to conveniently start the machine. Then another installation would be required inside the machine to run it. So that's tricky and maybe beyond the scope of `podman-py` (or at least I would subpackage this functionality and run something like `pip install podman[machine/utils]` to split it from the API package).

Definitely an interesting conversation to have about more `podman-py` functionalities and features for version 6

Thanks for the detailed answer - it makes a lot of sense that `podman machine` is only relevant for macOS and Windows.
I also see your point (and agree) that podman-py defines itself as a `library of bindings to use the RESTful API`.

> Where does podman-py fit? Say that you install podman-py on Windows to conveniently start the machine. Then another installation would be required inside the machine to run it.

I may be missing something here, but doesn't a Podman installation exist in the VM already, that exposes an API that the Podman binary in the host connects to? In this scenario, I would assume that `podman-py` could connect to that API as well.

In any case, for the needs of a Python project we're working on ([Dangerzone](https://github.com/freedomofpress/dangerzone)) we need to issue `podman machine` commands across multiple platforms (Windows / macOS). Currently, we don't have a better way to do that than create a Pythonic interface over the `podman` binary, and issue `podman machine` commands under the hood. I guess if that interface is generic enough, we could contribute it to `podman-py` (or a `podman[machine/utils]` feature), if there's interest from other `podman-py` users for it.

> I may be missing something here, but doesn't a Podman installation exist in the VM already, that exposes an API that the Podman binary in the host connects to? In this scenario, I would assume that podman-py could connect to that API as well.

yes podman is installed within `podman machine` vm.

> In any case, for the needs of a Python project we're working on ([Dangerzone](https://github.com/freedomofpress/dangerzone)) we need to issue podman machine commands across multiple platforms (Windows / macOS).

Please don't hesitate to summon me with a tag in dangerzone issues and I will take a look if I can help :)
Thanks! Will do, once I have the interface figured out and something to show. Hopefully soon 🙂
